prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(create_hs_code_support_request export_shipment_1)
(assign_classification_resource export_shipment_1 classification_tool_1)
(propose_hs_code export_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(confirm_proposed_hs_code export_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(allocate_evidence_template_to_export_shipment export_shipment_1 export_bucket_1 evidence_template_1)
(revoke_proposed_hs_code export_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(create_hs_code_support_request import_shipment_1)
(assign_classification_resource import_shipment_1 classification_tool_2)
(propose_hs_code import_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(confirm_proposed_hs_code import_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(assign_compliance_reviewer_to_case import_shipment_1 compliance_reviewer_1)
(detect_import_evidence_requirements import_shipment_1 import_bucket_1 hs_code_1)
(allocate_reviewer_for_import_shipment import_shipment_1 import_bucket_1 compliance_reviewer_1)
(release_reviewer_assignment import_shipment_1 compliance_reviewer_1)
(revoke_proposed_hs_code import_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(propose_hs_code export_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(assemble_bundle_with_export_evidence export_shipment_1 import_shipment_1 export_bucket_1 import_bucket_1 submission_bundle_1)
(apply_evidence_template_to_export_shipment export_shipment_1 export_bucket_1 hs_code_1 evidence_template_1)
(validate_submission_bundle_for_shipment submission_bundle_1 export_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(approve_export_shipment_for_clearance export_shipment_1 submission_bundle_1)
(approve_import_shipment_for_clearance import_shipment_1 submission_bundle_1)
(apply_permit_to_case_and_record export_shipment_1 permit_record_1 hs_code_1)
(propagate_classification_assignment_to_export_shipment export_shipment_1 classification_tool_1 permit_record_1)
(revoke_proposed_hs_code export_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(propose_hs_code import_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(apply_permit_to_case_and_record import_shipment_1 permit_record_1 hs_code_1)
(propagate_classification_assignment_to_import_shipment import_shipment_1 classification_tool_2 permit_record_1)
(revoke_proposed_hs_code import_shipment_1 hs_code_1)
(create_hs_code_support_request broker_profile_1)
(assign_classification_resource broker_profile_1 classification_tool_1)
(propose_hs_code broker_profile_1 hs_code_1)
(confirm_proposed_hs_code broker_profile_1 hs_code_1)
(instantiate_evidence_attachment_and_link_to_bundle broker_profile_1 evidence_attachment_1 submission_bundle_1)
(assign_commodity_document_type_to_broker_profile broker_profile_1 commodity_doc_type_1)
(link_commodity_doc_type_and_stage_broker_submission broker_profile_1 evidence_attachment_1 submission_bundle_1 hs_code_1 commodity_doc_type_1)
(assign_technical_certificate_to_broker_profile broker_profile_1 technical_certificate_type_1)
(assign_compliance_reviewer_to_case broker_profile_1 compliance_reviewer_1)
(broker_perform_attestation_with_technical_certificate_variant_1 broker_profile_1 technical_certificate_type_1 compliance_reviewer_1 evidence_attachment_1 submission_bundle_1)
(attach_regulatory_authority_statement_to_broker_profile broker_profile_1 regulatory_authority_statement_1)
(apply_regulatory_statement_with_export_flag broker_profile_1 regulatory_authority_statement_1 evidence_attachment_1 submission_bundle_1)
(attach_third_party_report_to_broker_submission broker_profile_1 third_party_report_type_1)
(bind_shipments_and_bundle_and_mark_documents_validated broker_profile_1 export_shipment_1 import_shipment_1 hs_code_1 third_party_report_type_1)
(complete_broker_finalization broker_profile_1)
(release_reviewer_assignment broker_profile_1 compliance_reviewer_1)
(unassign_technical_certificate_from_broker_profile broker_profile_1 technical_certificate_type_1)
(detach_regulatory_authority_statement_from_broker_profile broker_profile_1 regulatory_authority_statement_1)
(apply_permit_to_case_and_record broker_profile_1 permit_record_1 hs_code_1)
(propagate_classification_assignment_to_broker_profile broker_profile_1 classification_tool_1 permit_record_1)
(revoke_proposed_hs_code broker_profile_1 hs_code_1)
(create_hs_code_support_request broker_profile_2)
(assign_classification_resource broker_profile_2 classification_tool_3)
(propose_hs_code broker_profile_2 hs_code_1)
(confirm_proposed_hs_code broker_profile_2 hs_code_1)
(instantiate_evidence_attachment_and_link_to_bundle broker_profile_2 evidence_attachment_2 submission_bundle_1)
(assign_commodity_document_type_to_broker_profile broker_profile_2 commodity_doc_type_2)
(link_commodity_doc_type_and_stage_broker_submission broker_profile_2 evidence_attachment_2 submission_bundle_1 hs_code_1 commodity_doc_type_2)
(assign_technical_certificate_to_broker_profile broker_profile_2 technical_certificate_type_1)
(assign_compliance_reviewer_to_case broker_profile_2 compliance_reviewer_1)
(broker_perform_attestation_with_technical_certificate_variant_1 broker_profile_2 technical_certificate_type_1 compliance_reviewer_1 evidence_attachment_2 submission_bundle_1)
(attach_regulatory_authority_statement_to_broker_profile broker_profile_2 regulatory_authority_statement_1)
(apply_regulatory_statement_with_export_flag broker_profile_2 regulatory_authority_statement_1 evidence_attachment_2 submission_bundle_1)
(attach_third_party_report_to_broker_submission broker_profile_2 third_party_report_type_2)
(bind_shipments_and_bundle_and_mark_documents_validated broker_profile_2 export_shipment_1 import_shipment_1 hs_code_1 third_party_report_type_2)
(complete_broker_finalization broker_profile_2)
(release_reviewer_assignment broker_profile_2 compliance_reviewer_1)
(unassign_technical_certificate_from_broker_profile broker_profile_2 technical_certificate_type_1)
(detach_regulatory_authority_statement_from_broker_profile broker_profile_2 regulatory_authority_statement_1)
(apply_permit_to_case_and_record broker_profile_2 permit_record_1 hs_code_1)
(propagate_classification_assignment_to_broker_profile broker_profile_2 classification_tool_3 permit_record_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves preparing evidence for HS code support with export_shipment_1, import_shipment_1, and two broker profiles: broker_profile_1 and broker_profile_2. The goal is to make all four entities submission ready: export_shipment_1, import_shipment_1, broker_profile_1, and broker_profile_2.


## Preparing Entities for Submission  
- The task focuses on making export_shipment_1, import_shipment_1, broker_profile_1, and broker_profile_2 ready for HS code support submission.  
- The goal is to ensure all four entities meet the necessary requirements for export and import documentation.

## Preparing Entities for Submission  
- Each entity (export_shipment_1, import_shipment_1, broker_profile_1, broker_profile_2) requires a create_hs_code_support_request to start the process.  
- All entities must go through classification, proposing and confirming an HS code, and assigning a compliance reviewer.  

## Managing Limited Resources  
- Only one hs_code_1 and one compliance_reviewer_1 are available for all four entities, which may cause bottlenecks.  
- Three classification_tools are available, providing some flexibility for assigning classification resources.  

## Achieving Final Review  
- Shipments need approve_export_shipment_for_clearance or approve_import_shipment_for_clearance, requiring readiness for bundling and satisfied requirements.  
- Brokers achieve final review through actions like finalize_broker_submission_package, needing specific regulatory attachments.

## Managing Limited Resources  
- With only three classification_tools available and four entities to process, not all can be assigned simultaneously, creating a potential bottleneck.  
- To manage, one approach is to process entities sequentially, reusing tools after each entity reaches a stage where resources can be released, such as after propagation.  

## Handling HS Code Resource Constraints  
- The hs_code_1 resource is limited to one, and only one entity can propose it at a time due to availability constraints.  
- Revoking a proposed HS code releases the resource for reuse, but doing so removes the proposed status, which later actions (e.g., applying permits) may still require, creating conflicts.  

## Ensuring Process Continuity  
- Each entity must maintain its proposed HS code status through final review and propagation, as actions like detecting evidence requirements and validating bundles depend on it.  
- Proposing the same HS code for multiple entities sequentially is not feasible, as revoking for one entity would break later steps needing the proposed code for that entity.

## Preparing Shipments for Bundling  
- Allocating evidence templates to export shipments requires confirmation, bucket assignment, and available templates, marking shipments ready for bundling.  
- Applying evidence templates sets requirements as satisfied, making shipments eligible for bundle assembly, and releases template availability.  

## Validating Submission Bundles  
- Validating submission bundles for export shipments requires a prepared bundle, readiness for bundling, and a proposed HS code, marking the bundle as validated.  
- The validation process is specific to export shipments, with no separate action identified for import shipments in the domain.  

## Assembling and Approving Bundles  
- Assembling bundles requires shipments to be ready, with assigned buckets and satisfied requirements, using available bundle slots and marking bundles as prepared.  
- Approving shipments for clearance, both export and import, needs ready, satisfied, prepared, and validated statuses, ultimately setting final_passed.  

## Coordinating Broker Processes  
- Brokers require prepared and validated bundles for actions like instantiating evidence attachments, linking to the same bundle as shipments.  
- Both brokers, linked to the same shipments and bundle, need to reach submission_ready, potentially processed sequentially due to resource limits.

## Managing HS Code Allocation  
- Proposing an HS code is necessary for validating submission bundles, but it becomes unavailable for other shipments until revoked.  
- Revoking a proposed HS code frees it for reuse, like assigning it to an import shipment, but removes the proposed status needed for later validation steps.  

## Handling Validation Conflicts  
- Validating a bundle for export requires the shipment to have a proposed HS code, but revoking it for import use creates a catch-22, blocking validation.  
- Re-proposing the HS code to the export shipment after import use might resolve the issue, ensuring validation can proceed for both.

## Navigating HS Code Conflicts  
- Validating submission bundles requires an export shipment with a proposed HS code, but this blocks proposing to imports, creating a dependency loop.  
- Both export and import shipments need to be ready for bundling, but proposing an HS code to one makes it unavailable for the other, causing a deadlock.  

## Sequencing Bundle Preparation  
- To assemble a bundle, both shipments must be ready, but validating requires the export to have a proposed HS code, which may need re-proposing after revoking.  
- The sequence involves getting the export ready first, then revoking its HS code to propose to the import, but re-proposing to export later is needed for validation.  

## Resolving Readiness Deadlocks  
- Attempting to ready the import without proposing an HS code fails, as all steps like confirming and allocating reviewers require a proposed HS code.  
- The process seems stuck unless the HS code is revoked from export first, but this removes the proposed status needed for later validation, creating a catch-22.

## Exploring Template Use  
- Using evidence templates allows shipments to reach ready_for_bundling without needing a proposed HS code, focusing on confirmed status instead.  
- Allocating a template sets bucket_has_evidence and ready_for_bundling, bypassing the need for satisfaction requirements initially.  

## Assembling Without Satisfaction  
- Assembling a bundle with both evidences doesn't require shipments to be satisfied, only that both are ready and have evidence.  
- This approach enables bundle preparation by ensuring export and import buckets have evidence, using assemble_bundle_with_both_evidences action.  

## Sequencing HS Code Management  
- The process starts by proposing and confirming an HS code for export, then allocating a template to set readiness without further HS code proposals.  
- Revoking the HS code can free it for import, but may not be needed if validation paths rely on earlier confirmation rather than ongoing proposals.

## Exploring Hybrid Approaches  
- One approach is to process one shipment via reviewer steps (requiring applicable and proposed status) and the other via template allocation for has_evidence and readiness without satisfaction.  
- This hybrid strategy allows assembling bundles using actions like assemble_bundle_with_export_evidence, matching readiness and evidence conditions for both shipments.  

## Sequencing HS Code Proposals  
- Proposing HS codes for both shipments at different times, with revoking if needed, helps manage resource conflicts and readiness.  
- The sequence involves confirming export first, then using templates for readiness, ensuring validation requirements are met later.

## Managing HS Code Proposals  
- After allocating a template for export, revoking its proposed HS code frees it for import processing, ensuring readiness and satisfaction.  
- Import follows reviewer steps, proposing and confirming an HS code, then detecting evidence to become applicable and satisfied, while export remains ready with evidence but not satisfied.  

## Re-Proposing for Assembly  
- After import is ready, revoking its HS code and re-proposing it to export ensures export has a proposed status needed for assembly.  
- Assembling the bundle checks both are ready, export has evidence, import is applicable and satisfied, and a slot is available, setting the bundle as prepared.  

## Validating and Approving Challenges  
- Validation requires the export to be proposed, which is met after re-proposing, allowing the bundle to be validated successfully.  
- Approval for export fails as it requires satisfaction, which wasn't set earlier, revealing a gap in the process despite successful assembly.

## Examining Broker Pathways  
- For brokers like profile1, the process requires confirming entity classification, which involves creating and assigning a classification tool, proposing an HS code, and confirming it.  
- Resource constraints, such as limited availability of classification tools, may necessitate revoking HS codes from shipments to free them for broker use.  

## Managing HS Code Allocation  
- Export shipments, after reaching final_passed, still need a proposed HS code for applying permits, complicating the process if codes are revoked earlier.  
- Import shipments, currently without proposed HS codes, also require proposing again before applying permits, highlighting the need for careful sequencing.  

## Staging Documents for Brokers  
- Staging documents for broker attestation, such as stage_attachment_for_broker_attestation, requires the attachment to be instantiated and linked to the bundle, with no export evidence in the bundle.  
- The process checks if the broker has confirmed classification, proposed HS code, and attached documents, ensuring all preconditions are met before staging.

## Evaluating Submission Variants  
- Four variants exist for applying regulatory statements, varying by export/import flags and finalization outcomes.  
- With export flag (our case, bundle_has_export yes, has_import no), it sets additional_attestation_flag and finalizes with regulatory authority statement availability.  

## Sequencing Broker Processes  
- Using with_export_flag requires attaching a third-party report, necessitating third_party_report_type_1 or 2, both available for two brokers.  
- Binding shipments and marking documents validated needs export and import satisfaction, proposed HS codes, and linked brokers, all currently met.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Shared resources like regulatory statements and permits (permit_record_1 available) limit simultaneous processing, requiring sequential handling for multiple brokers.  
- Completing shipments to submission_ready involves applying permits and propagating classifications, but permit availability (one permit) necessitates sequencing.  

## Finalizing Shipment Readiness  
- Export shipment is proposed and can apply permit_record_1, moving to submission_ready after classification propagation.  
- Import shipment lacks a proposed HS code, requiring re-proposal before permit application, highlighting sequencing needs.

## Planning Shipment Steps  
- The process outlines 28 steps to finalize both export and import shipments, starting with creating HS code support requests for each.  
- Steps include assigning classification resources, proposing and confirming HS codes, and applying permits, with some actions like revoking proposed codes repeated for adjustments.  

## Finalizing Shipment Readiness  
- After initial steps, both shipments reach final_review_passed, then apply permits and propagate classifications to achieve submission_ready status.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
